fh.shot_strategy: With a 4 o-clock on Hole D, your RHFH is partially exposed — use a stable disc on a controlled anhyzer to manage the crosswind component over this 202ft par 3.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 4 o-clock on Hole D, your LHBH is vulnerable to the rightward push — use an overstable disc on a strong hyzer or prefer LHFH over this 202ft par 3.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 10 o-clock on Hole 16 (Yellow), your RHBH flight plate is fully exposed — prefer RHFH for this wind direction to keep the disc stable over this 289ft par 3. Watch for Out of Bounds on right side and bias your shot away from it.

fh.shot_strategy: With a 10 o-clock on Hole 16 (Yellow), your RHFH fade works naturally with this wind — use a stable to overstable disc for a reliable flight over this 289ft par 3. Watch for Out of Bounds on right side and bias your shot away from it.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 9 o-clock crosswind from left on Hole 18 (Yellow), your RHBH fade works with the wind pushing left — use a stable to overstable disc on a flat to slight hyzer release for a reliable fade over this 295ft par 3. Watch for Out of Bounds on right side and bias your shot away from it.

Watch for Out of Bounds on right side and bias your shot away from it.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 9 o-clock crosswind from left on Hole 18 (Yellow), your LHBH natural fade goes right, wind pushes left — they work together, use a stable to overstable disc on a flat release over this 295ft par 3. Watch for Out of Bounds on right side and bias your shot away from it.
